title: Which volumes of the manga are difficult to get
display_title: Which volumes of the manga are difficult to get
wikitext:
[]: Which volumes of the manga are difficult to get?             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Nov 14, 2011 8:53 am Can someone tell me or direct me to the thread(s) that say as much? Ta.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 14, 2011 9:01 am Basically what has happened is the manga has been drawn out over such a long period of time, it's impractical to keep in print. So what Viz has been doing for the past 5 years is simply doing a limited run of each volume when it's released, based upon an estimation of demand. Sometimes the estimate is good, but with the case of volume 9, it was particularly low, so it's mostly volume 9 that's difficult to find: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6955/Manga-Why-is-Volume-9-of-the-manga-so-friggin-rare/ If you're considering collecting the manga, I recommend you either wait a while for it to be completed, and given a full, new printing, or try getting a lot of the entire set (including volume 9) from eBay.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Nov 14, 2011 9:26 am Another way is to get the Japanese manga (which has never got out of print) and check the translation on the net.

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Mon Nov 14, 2011 9:33 am Last time I checked (which was yesterday)all of the eva manga was on amazon.ca for sale all of which are new including vol 9 (for ten dollars). Downside is that most of them won't ship for 2-6 months at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 14, 2011 9:51 am symbv wrote: Another way is to get the Japanese manga (which has never got out of print) and check the translation on the net.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Nov 14, 2011 9:53 am The Eva Monkey wrote: People are more likely to resort to piracy at that point.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Nov 14, 2011 11:09 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Sometimes the estimate is good, but with the case of volume 9, it was particularly low, so it's mostly volume 9 that's difficult to find: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6955/Manga-Why-is-Volume-9-of-the-manga-so-friggin-rare/

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Nov 14, 2011 11:47 am Jayfive wrote: Ta, thats the thread I had in mind when I started the thread, though I had it in my head it was vol 6 that was the rare one for some reason. Volume 9 on amazon UK: 2 new from £124.36 3 used from £124.04 Ill keep my eye out on ebay for the volumes generally, should be able to pick most of them up for buttons. Eva is nowhere as big here in Blighty as it is in the US (i reckon mainly because its never been on TV like the US had NGE on Adult Swim) so the demand is not quite the same.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Mon Nov 14, 2011 10:52 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: If you're going to resort to another region's release, there are other English releases, notably the Australian release.

[]:             Quote Post by insomniac » Tue Nov 15, 2011 6:04 am ^ Same here, our Borders had the Chuang Yi edition of Vol.9 locally before it closed down too, though it seems to be the only edition they stock. But I never bought them because I hated the design of the masthead.
